title: Reverse Proxies, Forwarded Fields, Framing, and Request Smuggling Defense · Stakeholder TypeScript Mission
summary: A payment and fulfillment integration team. It needs exact CORS and CSRF policy, secure cookies and headers, signed replay-resistant webhooks, bounded egress, business-abuse controls, trusted forwarding, parser consistency, and drain evidence. Planned artifact: Deliver Browser-Safe Webhook and Proxy Gateway with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Protect cookie-authenticated mutations with safe-method semantics, tokens, trusted Origin or Referer, Fetch Metadata, and explicit exceptional-client handling.
Deploy CSP, frame-ancestors, HSTS at the correct authority, MIME sniffing prevention, referrer policy, permissions policy, and cache controls with browser tests.
Deduplicate stable delivery identifiers, persist acceptance atomically, return promptly, process asynchronously, and classify retryable provider responses.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Project Csrf Token Origin Fetch Metadata
steps.instruction:
project case — A project team handles case 2987 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reject untrusted topology, ambiguous framing, and insecure parser policy; preserve one tenant fairness invariant; then fail the canary while the old revision drains and prove rollback remains safe. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?
